key_finding: V-MAGNITUDE SHOWS CLIFF ON QWEN AT sigma=3-5 (100/100 at sigma<=3, then 50% at sigma=5, 20% at sigma=10). Digital encoding extends to BOTH K and V on Qwen (K cliff at sigma=0.3-1, V cliff at sigma=3-5). Superadditivity WEAK on Qwen (1.1-2.0x) vs Llama's 7.9-16.8x — staggered cliffs prevent cascading failure. V-mag sigma=1 immunity now 149/149 cross-model.
evidence_impact: Completes the cross-model dose-response characterization. Digital/analog distinction applies to BOTH K and V components, not just K. Superadditivity is encoding-strategy-specific: overlapping gradual degradation (Llama) creates cascading failure, staggered cliffs (Qwen) don't. V more robust than K confirmed at ALL doses on BOTH models.
next_planned: Priority: (1) V-mag cliff resolution on Qwen (sigma=4 to characterize cliff precisely). (2) Mechanism: WHY is V cliff at 5-10x higher sigma than K? Attention norm analysis. (3) Third model family for dose-response. (4) K-only PGD (exploit K-direction null space directly). (5) Practical implications: test if AC-aware compression preserves V-norms and K-directions.

positional_dissociation.status: STRONG: universal across models. Late positions carry answer info via K-routing

geometric_dissociation.status: K-V dissociation CROSS-MODEL REPLICATED. K cliff (sigma 0.3-1) and V cliff (sigma 3-5) on Qwen. Gradual K/V on Llama

natural_usage.status: H2O/TC protection via position. R-KV shows removing redundant tokens IMPROVES reasoning

cross_model_replication.status: COMPLETE dose-response on 2 families. V-mag immunity 149/149. K-V direction ratio 3.6-3.7x. Need 3rd family

encoding_strategy.status: Digital (Qwen: K+V cliffs) vs analog (Llama: K+V gradual). Digital/analog applies to BOTH K and V. V threshold 5-10x higher than K on Qwen

superadditive_interaction.status: Llama-specific (7.9-16.8x). Weak on Qwen (1.1-2.0x). Explained by staggered vs overlapping degradation windows

literature_convergence.status: 5+ independent groups converge. K-V dissociation is novel contribution beyond existing literature
